name: Sound Lure
description: A sound event that attracts, distracts, redirects, or escalates monster behavior based on source location, volume profile, occlusion, and AI state.
design_notes: Sound lures give players agency over fear when the monster can hear and react. The system should define noise source ids, radius, occlusion, priority, active window, monster reaction, and readable audio cues. Lures should create risky opportunities, not solve every patrol pattern for free.
design_purpose: Let players redirect or bait monsters without direct combat.
Connect inventory items, environmental objects, stealth mistakes, and AI investigation.
Create tactical tradeoffs between making progress quietly and creating a noisy opening.
player_fantasy: The player feels they can manipulate danger with sound, but every noise might also make the situation worse.

parameters.name: noise_event_id
parameters.description: Noise event emitted into AI perception.

parameters.name: sound_lure_id
parameters.description: Item, device, or environmental source producing the lure.

parameters.name: source_position_id
parameters.description: World-space source used by monster pathing and debug display.

parameters.name: lure_radius
parameters.description: Perception radius before occlusion and alert modifiers.

parameters.name: lure_active_window_ms
parameters.description: Window during which the lure can attract or hold attention.

parameters.name: occlusion_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule for how level geometry changes perceived noise.

parameters.name: monster_reaction_id
parameters.description: AI behavior selected after perception priority resolves.

edge_cases: A lure is activated while the monster already has direct line of sight to the player.
Several noises fire at once and need deterministic priority resolution.
The lure lands somewhere reachable to the player but unreachable to the monster navmesh.
A door closes after the lure activates and changes occlusion or pathing.
A safe room boundary blocks monster movement but the noise source is inside the safe room.
common_bugs: The monster runs to the player throw origin instead of the lure source position.
Audio playback succeeds but no noise_event_id is sent to AI perception.
Occlusion lowers the audible volume visually but not in AI logic.
Thrown lure objects despawn before the monster reaches the source, clearing investigation state early.
Debug visualization shows the raw radius and hides wall-damped or door-blocked perception.
balancing_notes: Reliable lures empower stealth play; unreliable lures increase fear but can feel unfair without clear rules.
Consumable lures can be stronger than reusable noise sources because inventory scarcity limits abuse.
Monsters should not abandon a high-confidence chase for every small lure unless the lure is designed as a strong escape tool.
accessibility_notes: Provide visual indicators or subtitles for important lure sounds and monster reactions.
Avoid requiring precise audio direction alone to understand whether a lure worked.
Let players remap or simplify throw and placement controls for lure devices.

implementation_notes.unity: Emit noise_event_id from the authoritative impact or activation point, not from the input origin. Use physics or navmesh checks for reachable source positions, apply occlusion_rule_id before AI priority, and draw debug spheres for perceived lure radius.
implementation_notes.godot: Create a noise event resource containing source_position_id, lure radius, active window, and reaction hints. Send it to monster AI through a perception manager so audio playback and AI response cannot drift apart.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use AI Perception hearing stimuli or custom gameplay events with source actor, loudness profile, and Gameplay Tags. Behavior Trees should compare lure priority against current chase state and log why the monster investigated or ignored the lure.
implementation_notes.web: In Phaser or custom engines, represent sound lures as gameplay events with expiry and source coordinates. Process AI reactions in fixed decision steps and render debug overlays for radius, occlusion, and selected monster_reaction_id.

example_games.title: Alien: Isolation
example_games.note: Uses noise-making tools to influence monster attention.

example_games.title: Amnesia: The Bunker
example_games.note: Connects noise, exploration, and monster response as survival pressure.
